query: latest academic integrity guidelines for AI tools in college classrooms

url_quality_notes.https://www.facebook.com/groups/14934829: Low-authority forum post (authority 0.2, relevance 0.3), truncated to 25 words, shares identical snippet with Harvard page suggesting content mirroring; should not appear in top results for this query.
url_quality_notes.https://oaisc.fas.harvard.edu/academic-i: High-authority official Harvard page (authority 0.95, relevance 0.9) with full policy coverage but scrape_score=0.20 is anomalously low given complete content; lacks publication date.
url_quality_notes.https://www.apa.org/ed/precollege/psycho: Credible APA source (authority 0.95) with verifiable 2025-08-14 publication date; only source confirming 'latest' temporal requirement but less policy-specific than Harvard/Cornell.
url_quality_notes.https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PM: Peer-reviewed article (authority 0.75) but from 2024-10-24 (not latest) and provides theoretical framework rather than actionable classroom guidelines; scrape_score=0.20 is anomalously low.
url_quality_notes.https://teaching.cornell.edu/generative-: Highest query_relevance_score (0.95) with specific policy frameworks (AI policy icons, evidence standards); authority 0.9; but lacks publication date and has boilerplate noise in scrape.

query: evidence based diagnostic criteria and assessment methods for adult ADHD

url_quality_notes.https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PM: Excellent: peer-reviewed AQAS framework with DSM-5-TR/ICD-11 criteria, adult symptom thresholds, semi-structured interview guidance. scrape_level=L1, relevance=1.0.
url_quality_notes.https://www.cdc.gov/adhd/diagnosis/index: Poor relevance: child-focused ADHD diagnosis page with critical content gaps for adult ADHD. answers_query=false, coverage_level=tangential. Should not rank above adult-specific sources.
url_quality_notes.https://www.psychiatry.org/patients-fami: Good: APA official page covering adult ADHD symptoms, evaluation overview, and comorbidity. Partial coverage — lacks detailed DSM-5 criteria and specific validated tools. nav_link_ratio=0.85 is high.
url_quality_notes.https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/ar: Excellent: peer-reviewed systematic review identifying validated adult ADHD screening tools with psychometric data. relevance=0.95. Should rank above CDC and APA.
url_quality_notes.https://www.therapylab.com/blogs/adult-a: Moderate: commercial blog with partial coverage, references DSM-5 but lacks detailed criteria and specific validated instruments. authority_score=0.6.

query: latest clinical guidelines for managing long COVID symptoms in adults

url_quality_notes.https://www.cdc.gov/long-covid/hcp/clini: Authoritative CDC guideline, complete text, March 2026, query_relevance=0.98. Minor scrape issue with images not rendered but textual content fully preserved. Correctly ranked #1.
url_quality_notes.https://www.uptodate.com/contents/covid-: Scrape completely failed — only 3-word loading placeholder captured (scrape_score=0.20). Would be highly relevant if content were extracted but currently provides zero value. Should be demoted or retried with JS rendering.
url_quality_notes.https://www.acponline.org/clinical-infor: Authoritative ACP resource hub with links to external guidelines, but does not contain full clinical protocols itself. Partially relevant (query_relevance=0.7). Reasonable at rank 3.
url_quality_notes.https://www.idsociety.org/practice-guide: Completely irrelevant to long COVID — this is an acute COVID-19 treatment guideline (query_relevance=0.0). Should not appear in results for this query. 52K words of off-topic content.
url_quality_notes.https://publications.ersnet.org/content/: Most query-relevant result (query_relevance=1.0) with 10 evidence-based long COVID recommendations from a multidisciplinary international panel. March 2026. Only abstract extracted — full text in PDF. Should be ranked #2 behind CDC.
